raw_input:
[5.0/5] Multiple uses for cold therapy This little tool is going to see many types of use in my home. I suffer from migraines, so cold therapy is my go to for those. In addition to headaches, we also have out share of muscle aches and bumps and bruises. This tool will be great for giving those localized attention without the bulk of an ice pack. We can also control the pressure applied.

The two heads are easily popped on and off. It's nice that you get two, because you can always have a spare in the freezer, and switch them out when needed. This is also going to be a great cool down tool for my husband after doing yardwork. The Texas sun is brutal in the summertime. Applying this to his face and the back of his neck is going to be a real treat. I think that with the multiple uses this provides, it's worth grabbing one. [1.0/5] Roller barely rotates, you'd basically get same effect by keeping some metal spoons in your freezer If this device had a motor in the handle that caused the cold head to rotate, there'd be more to talk about here, but frankly this is just two metal discs you can put in your freezer with cheaply made plastic attachment pieces to stick on a small cheap handle. It doesn't rotate on its own, you have to do that for it, and when you try to, the poor connection between the cold metal heads and their plastic bases and the handles doesn't even produce consistent spins the way it's supposed to.

You'd honestly be better served by just keeping two metal spoons in your freezer and using them the same way: put it on your skin and move it in a circular motion. If you find it's slightly too cold, wrap a paper towel square around the spoon head and rubber band it in place. Same effect.

***Overall Value***

This is a pretty blatant cash grab that offers little no nothing for what you're paying. If there was a motor in the handle to rotate the head, that would be one thing, but this is akin to selling a normal toothbrush and asking for 3 times as much many saying it's like an electric toothbrush because you can move it in a circular motion with your wrist and make your own humming sound.

Save your money, go put some spoons in your freezer. Apply to face as needed. [4.0/5] Soothing I thought this vibrated as well, but what you see is what you get. It comes with a head and replacement head with covers for easy storing. A nice way to apply my moisturizer. [4.0/5] Refreshing This ice roller feels refreshing on my eye area, it the part of my face more affected due to seasonal allergies, so, the cold gives me a soothing an calming effect, it also feels relaxing. [5.0/5] So soothing! I got this to use on my face, but the day after it came in the mail, I pulled a ligament in my knee. This face roller was the perfect size and shape to apply cold to my injury! It was very comfortable to use, didn't 'burn' my skin like when I use plain ice or ice packs, and since it is smaller and curved, I could really apply the cold to the area in the crevice of my knee where the pain was.

I did use it on my face the next day, and that was great, too. But I'm still using it for my knee after a long day of standing, and it works wonders! [5.0/5] Alleviated puffiness Love this ice massager! It’s very lightweight and I like having the two interchangeable heads. I put both of them in the freezer and used one. And if you still need it, you can swap it out for the other from the freezer. Its size allows you to target the under eye area easily. And it stays cold for quite a while. Perfect for those mornings when you need a little extra help to look refreshed. [5.0/5] Helps with puffiness This has really helped alot with the puffiness under my eyes I use it 2 or 3 times a day depending on how puffy my eyes look but mainly before bed and in the morning and so fare its helped alot my eyes aren't really that puffy anymore but ill keep using so I can make sure it dont come back I never knew something like this could really work.
Oh and it only takes like 15 minutes in freezer and its cold for like a hour and when you think its not cold anymore just stop and give it a second and then put back on your face you will see its still cold that your face just warmed the outside a bit but this really is amazing how this works. [5.0/5] Facial Ice Roller Ice Roller for Face & Eye, Face Roller with A Replaceable Head for Reducing Puffiness, Migraine, Pain Relief & Minor Injury.

Straight out of the box, I was impressed with how cold the head felt without being placed in the cooler. So smooth when gliding along on cheeks and forehead. Once chilled, I was even more impressed. The coldness was a little jarring on my sinuses, but I can definitely see how beneficial it would be to reduce a migraine or headache. Most excellent on under-eye bags. Invigorating and refreshing, a quick wake-me-up.

Love the white and teal color scheme, the handle a nice size to grip to control the movements. I appreciate that this set came with two heads, so quick and easy to change them out and pop back into the cooler. [4.0/5] Awesome massager! [[VIDEOID:9afbc03a2aa79005b3f61c7ab46c06a0]] A great cool massager for sure- it comes with an extra head which is convenient. It is well made and it cools quickly - it can be used on face to alleviate puffiness and tighten the skin! It can be used on injuries too.

I also used it on my tired eyes after too much device time and believe me it helped cook my eyes instantly- you can apply cream on face and massage over that too!

I am very happy with this little device!!

Highly recommended! [4.0/5] LOVE Love this! You would think putting this on your face while cold would be too much but its just right! Love using it on my face! the only thing I dont like is that the attachments sometimes come off while using it. I dont like that but it is still usable and I love it [5.0/5] Soooo much better than an ice pack I've had migraines since I was a kid and have never lived anywhere where my freezer didn't have numerous ice packs for migraines to help ease the pain and bring down the temperature.

While I wont be using this to try to drop my body temp which shoots up with a migraine. I will absolutely use this to ease the pain - it feels delightful rubbed across my temple, between my eyes, etc. All of those places where you migraine is felt to the touch.

Genius product. [5.0/5] Love love this item! Love this item! Ive been using the ice stick style that melts… or the glass ball that you freeze… this one is my favorite. So more sturdy & no leaking water down my face. I use this every morning now!

Also comes w 2 ice parts so youre never without! ☺️ [5.0/5] This is super nice and came with an extra head to make sure you never run out of icy power! Has an extra head to make sure you keep icing. Also it is super sturdy and feels good on the face. Better than the last one I tried. Much better quality! [4.0/5] Ice Roller for Face & Eye - Great relief for burning eyes due to allergies. Anyone who suffers with allergies knows just how red and itchy it makes the eyes, not to mention the pain due to rubbing them raw. This little eye roller is absolutely fantastic for relief. Just pop the heads into the freezer, remove one, attach it to the roller machine thingy and gently glide it over the eyes. Once it warms, pop off, get the cold one out of the freezer, put the warm one in it and repeat as often as needed. It's saved my poor eyes. [2.0/5] Doesn't really roll. Comes with handle and 2 ice rollers (with plastic for the metal portion of the roller). The rollers push in or pull out of handle. The metal part that gets cold is 1.75" and round. There is nothing to keep the coves on the rollers. Have to store vertical or back in box that it came in. To use, just put metal ice rollers into freezer/fridge to make the cold and then put onto handle for use. These rotate and are really hard to make them roll/rotate. Most of the time, they kind of slide on your face or forehead. Think it is a bad design for a "roller". Can kind of use just for putting cold metal round part on face or eye area so I guess not totally useless. Only 2 stars and don't recommend. [4.0/5] Cooling is relaxing I found that this is a very easy wand to operate. If you put one head in the freezer or fridge, you always have one ready.

It's a nice size and fits my hands well. I have found that around "that time of the month", my eyes are puffier and this seems to help with the inflammation.

I think my only issue with it is it's not a true "roller". It's more of a wand and it doesn't really roll over your face. Otherwise, it feels nice and does seem to help reduce inflammation.

response:
Most buyers happy. Good for migraines, puffiness, allergies, injuries, eye bags. Two interchangeable heads big plus - keep one freezer, swap when needed. Stays cold long time. Lightweight, good grip, easy use. Works face, eyes, neck, knees, temples. Helps cool down hot weather too. Color scheme nice.

Two buyers unhappy. Main complaint: roller barely spins. Poor connection between cold metal heads and plastic handle. One say just use frozen spoons instead - same effect, save money. Another say hard rotate, design bad, but metal part still cold so not totally useless.

Overall most find worth buying. Best for puffiness, migraines, allergy eye relief. Two heads very convenient. Cold lasts good while in freezer.
